title: In /Users/masayuki/Dev/cursorvers/claude-config, verify test coverage/evidence for the Phase 0 handoff pipeline change. Compare against the 7-item test plan and acceptance criteria in ~/.claude/cache/handoff-phase0-codex.md and ~/.claude/cache/dev-spec-phase0-handoff-pipeline.md. Return missing test...
summary_text: Concrete regression bug is **verify-time script invocation mismatch**: - `record-provider` auto-attest path verifies via `bash "${ATTESTATION_SCRIPT}" verify ...`, so attestations can be created/marked verified at write time. [kernel-runtime-ledger.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/lib/kernel-runtime-ledger.sh#L271) [kernel-runtime-ledger.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/lib/kernel-runtime-ledger.sh#L277) - But `verified-provider-success-count` re-verifies via Python `subprocess.run([verifier, "verify", ...])` (direct exec, no `bash`). Since `scripts/lib/kernel-provider-attestation.sh` is not executable (`-rw-r--r--`), this raises `PermissionError`, so the command exits nonzero. [kernel-runtime-ledger.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/lib/kernel-runtime-ledger.sh#L353) [kernel-runtime-ledger.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/lib/kernel-runtime-ledger.sh#L368) [kernel-provider-attestation.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/lib/kernel-provider-attestation.sh) - Callers suppress that failure and coerce to `0`, which is why tests report missing provider evidence (`codex runtime evidence missing...`) even with `KERNEL_PROVIDER_TEST_AUTO_ATTEST=true`. [kernel-consensus-evidence.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/lib/kernel-consensus-evidence.sh#L96) [kernel-runtime-health.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/lib/kernel-runtime-health.sh#L158)
